_doc: v0.4 one-hop noValue check on hasNextStage. Fires on the missing successor in triggering_two_hop (preparation lacks hasNextStage). Currently this case actually works in Jena — listing here as a sanity-check baseline that v0.5 doesn't regress.
rationale: One-hop check is sufficient for single-gap chains. Multi-hop transitive closure (e.g., 5+ stages with gaps in different positions) is where pure Jena would degrade — out of scope for v0.5 brittleness suite.
